mapping.a_element: master-equation description of a system coupled to its environment (GKLS)
mapping.b_element: stochastic dynamics of a finite population subject to demographic noise

mapping.a_element: relaxation to a non-canonical steady state (thermalisation)
mapping.b_element: settling into a long-lasting polymorphic state
demographic noise strength in the finite population

brief_justification: The only overlap is generic — both describe a stochastic system relaxing to a nontrivial long-time state — but the governing machinery differs: paper A is a quantum dissipative GKLS master equation whose specific content is a detailed-balance analogue and coupling constants driving thermalisation, whereas paper B is demographic-noise-driven evolutionary game dynamics analysed by timescale separation to explain noise-induced polymorphism, and A's central elements (coupling strengths, detailed balance, experimental parameter extraction) have no role-for-role counterpart in B's noise-versus-selection mechanism.
